question:
(a)Determine the sample size required for conducting a two-sided test of $\mathrm{H}_0$ : $p_1=p_2$ with an equal number of subjects in each group, $\alpha=0.05 \left(z_{1-(.05 / 2)}=1.96\right), 1-\beta=0.90\left(z_{.90}=1.282\right), p_1=0.10$, and $p_2=0.05$.
(b)How many individuals should be studied if we want to incorporate a continuity correction into our test statistic?
answer:
(a)$$
 ar{p}= rac{p_1+p_2}{2}= rac{0.10+0.05}{2}=0.075 ;  ar{q}=1- ar{p}=1-0.075=0.925
$$

$$
 egin{aligned}
n & =\left( rac{z_{1-1} \sqrt{2  ar{p}  ar{q}}+z_1- eta \sqrt{p_1 q_1+p_2 q_2}}{\left|p_1-p_2ight|}ight)^2 \
& =\left( rac{1.96 \sqrt{2(0.075)(0.925)}+1.282 \sqrt{(0.10)(0.90)+(0.05)(0.95)}}{|0.10-0.05|}ight)^2 \
& =581.26
\end{aligned}
$$


Resolve to use 582 per group.
(b)$$
	ext { Solution: }  egin{aligned}
n^{\prime} & = rac{n}{4}\left(1+\sqrt{1+ rac{4}{n\left|p_1-p_2ight|}}ight)^2 \
& = rac{581.26}{4}\left(1+\sqrt{1+ rac{4}{581.26|0.10-0.05|}}ight)^2 \
& =620.6
\end{aligned}
$$


Therefore, resolve to study 621 observations per group.
